---
title: "Walkthrough notebook"
description: "The hand-built Nunn–Trefler analysis the automated pipeline later reproduced bit-for-bit."
---

Before the pipeline existed, the whole RECAST method was first done **by hand** on
Nunn & Trefler (2010): replicate the original OLS result, port the Baiardi–Naghi
DML extension, build the gap table, and add an exploratory heterogeneity analysis —
all in one annotated Jupyter notebook. That notebook is the methodology's
ground truth: when the automated pipeline was later run on the same paper, it
reproduced this hand-built analysis **bit-for-bit** (all 21 DML cells, zero drift).

::: {.callout-tip}
## Download the walkthrough
A self-contained, executed notebook that walks the full
replicate → extend → gap-table analysis end to end.

[Download the notebook (.ipynb)](../downloads/recast_phase1_nunn_trefler.ipynb){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

The automated counterpart — same numbers, produced by the pipeline and put through
the two-referee review — is the [Nunn & Trefler paper
page](../papers/nunn_trefler_2010/). Comparing the two is the cleanest way to see
that the automation introduced no methodological drift.
